# MSG / Email Parser — Usage Guide

*Mode: direct · 🔌 offline (no LLM)*

Parses an Outlook .msg or standard .eml email into structured JSON: sender, date, subject, body text and the list of attachments. Pure deterministic extraction — no LLM, fully offline.

## When to use it

Use this when you need an email's fields as data (for a script, a record, or further processing) rather than reading it in a mail client.

## Inputs

- **Email file (.msg or .eml)** (required) — accepts: Outlook .msg or RFC-822 .eml — one file.
  - The email file to parse.
  - ⚠️ Provide the email file itself; this does not connect to a mailbox.

## How to run

1. Select the .msg or .eml file.
2. Click Run.
3. Download the JSON.

## Output

Output is written to the **`Data/outputs/`** folder, named:

```
Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-email-parsed.json
```

Files produced:

- **`YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-email-parsed.json`** — Structured JSON with sender, date, subject, body text and an attachments list — easy to read into any script or tool.

## Tips

Attachments are listed (names), so you can see what was enclosed without opening the mail.

## Troubleshooting

| If… | Then… |
|------|-------|
| Body text looks empty. | Some HTML-only emails store the body differently; check the JSON fields — the text may be under the HTML body. |
